channel.title: Journal.ie – Daily 9-at-9 Mirror
channel.description: Locally mirrored feed of TheJournal.ie Daily 9-at-9 bulletins.

item.title: The 9 at 9: Saturday
item.description: Storm Dave approaches Ireland, US military planes shot down, and Artemis is more than halfway to the Moon.

item.title: The 9 at 9: Good Friday
item.description: Wind warning imminent, Trump makes more threats, and politicians call for free public transport.

item.title: The 9 at 9: Thursday
item.description: Trump tells Americans the war will soon be over, Enniscrone residents face evictions, and a new NASA rocket blasts off.

item.title: The 9 at 9: Tuesday
item.description: Dodgy boxes, Helen McEntree in Kyiv, and details of savings and investments plan to be outlined.

item.title: The 9 at 9: Sunday
item.description: Woman and teen charged over assault of Scarlett Faulker, US marines arrive in Middle East and the clocks spring forward.

item.title: The 9 at 9: Thursday
item.description: Trump claims Iran ‘afraid to say’ it wants deal, inflation could rose due to energy crisis, and Irish descend on Prague for crucial play-off.

item.title: The 9 at 9: Wednesday
item.description: Unresolved bicycle theft, fuel excise cuts, and proposed ceasefire plans for the Middle East.

item.title: The 9 at 9: Tuesday
item.description: Government to sign off on cuts to fuel taxes, Iran launches fresh strikes on Israel and EU strikes trade deal with Australia.

item.title: The 9 at 9: Monday
item.description: Iran vows Strait of Hormuz closure after Trump threats, gardaí test anti-drone tech and tributes paid to Michael Lyster.

item.title: 9 at 9: Saturday
item.description: Israel strikes Beirut and Tehran, Tánaiste hints at cut to excise duty, and planners give their view on illegal Meath mansion.

item.title: The 9 at 9: Friday
item.description: Israel says Iran is being ‘decimated,’ Amazon moves ahead with job cuts in Ireland and remission period for cancer survivors seeking mortgage protection reduced to five years

item.title: The 9 at 9: Thursday
item.description: Gas field in Iran attacked, hauliers meet with minister on fuel prices and Claire’s stores closing.

item.title: The 9 at 9: Wednesday
item.description: Iran vows revenge for killing of security chief, targeted vaccination programme in Kent after meningitis outbreak kills two and man in serious condition after being assaulted in Limerick

item.title: The 9 at 9: Monday
item.description: Jessie Buckley makes history in Los Angles as Irish Americans in Boston give their views on Trump and the Taoiseach.

item.title: The 9 at 9: Sunday
item.description: Iran vows to ‘target and kill’ Netanyahu, Taoiseach US trip continues and Irish hopefuls head to Academy Awards.

item.title: The 9 at 9: Saturday
item.description: Woman found dead in Cork named locally, Iran threatens US-linked oil facilities and North Korea fires ballistic missiles towards Sea of Japan

item.title: The 9 at 9: Friday
item.description: US moves to lift Russian oil sanctions in response to Gulf closure, Keir Starmer in Cork and a red flag for Ireland’s vegetable farms.

item.title: The 9 at 9: Thursday
item.description: Taoiseach to meet Keir Starmer in Cork, drivers crossing the border for fuel, and new tax relief for pharmacy services.

item.title: The 9 at 9: Wednesday
item.description: US destroys Iran’s minelayers in Strait of Hormuz, Wexford residents to press on with RTB cases and No 10 to begin publishing ‘Mandelson files’.

item.title: 9 at 9: Tuesday
item.description: Iran vows to fight on, Irish court rooms seeing violent incidents, and Taoiseach to meet with Spanish Prime Minister.

item.title: The 9 at 9: Monday
item.description: Iran fires missiles at Israel and Gulf nations, woman dies after being struck by car in Tipperary and civil trial against Gerry Adams to begin in London

item.title: The 9 at 9: Saturday
item.description: Iran rejects surrender to Israel and US, Irish ex-army chief says war ‘appears unlawful’ and a look at the landlord firm behind Wexford evictions.

item.title: The 9 at 9: Friday
item.description: CMAT wins the top prize at the Choice Music Awards, Dublin south-side train services resume and Trump demands a say in Iran’s next leader…

item.title: The 9 at 9: Thursday
item.description: More strikes across Iran, Israel and Lebanon, Irish arrive home from Dubai and our readers share reality of renting.

item.title: The 9 at 9: Wednesday
item.description: More strikes across Iran, Lebanon and Israel, Irish charter flight from UAE confirmed and Ireland’s special forces unit considering puclic recruitment.

item.title: The 9 at 9: Tuesday
item.description: US-Israel war with Iran could be ‘longer than a month’, Irish in UAE watch war unfold and Enoch Burke transferred from Mountjoy

item.title: The 9 at 9: Monday
item.description: Israel strikes Lebanon after Hezbollah fire, Jessie Buckley wins tops prize at Actor Awards and the experiences of Irish train commuters…

item.title: The 9 at 9: Sunday
item.description: Ayatollah Khamenei dead, thousands of flights cancelled across Middle East and Irish urged not to travel.

item.title: The 9 at 9: Saturday
item.description: US begins combat in Iran, concern for Galway renters, and Covid compensation.

item.title: The 9 at 9: Thursday
item.description: Clintons appear before US Epstein probe, Sinn Féin picks by-election candidate and Ireland needs a lot more construction workers.
